ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Nov-28-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laurence Blau, via Zoom, for and with Ted Maschler and Lauren Mullen, co-Administrators.

The court and counsel discuss the issues as advised to him by the Examiner regarding the tangible personal property. Counsel confirms the sale of some of decedent's personal property and that decedent's automobile was sold for about $6k and of a related auto insurance payment. Counsel confirms that all charges paid from decedent's credit card were incurred before death. The Court advises counsel that it can only allow, but not approve, an accounting that does not balance. No objection to the preceding is presented. The court confirms with counsel that the filing fee for this petition was $435 not $450. Counsel advises that a notice of proposed action was filed on Aug-26-2021. Counsel confirms that this Estate is not in the position to close due to unresolved tax issues.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
